We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:
The CEO to conduct project inspection at year-end, regardless of when the billing and SWA are submitted by the contractor, and to prepare a project accomplishment report, with copies furnished to the General Services Office and CAO, to serve as the basis for updating the Ledger Cards and Property Cards for Construction in Progress and Property, Plant, and Equipment accounts;
The CAO to update the CIP account as of year-end by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the 6 (six) completed projects totaling ₱13,875,447.35, adjust the CY 2024 CIP year-end balance by ₱157,286,399.81 for ten (10) ongoing projects, and record the related Accounts Payable for the same amount, based on the inspection and status of project accomplishment report prepared by the CEO; and
The CAO to compute and record Depreciation Expenses for the six completed projects from the date the assets were made available for use.

We recommended that the LCE direct:
The CHO-SO and other staff of the CHO Program to immediately conduct reconciliation of their records in order to trace the abovementioned unaccounted inventories, and prepare and submit the inventory reports along with the supporting documents to the GSO;
The GSO to immediately submit to the CAO the RSMI, along with the supporting documents for the drugs and medicines issued in CY 2024; and
The CAO to prepare a JEV to record the drugs and medicine issued in CY 2024.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the City Accountant to enhance the related party disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements by including the total remuneration received by KMP and the number of individuals in each KMP category, as well as the remuneration and compensation of their close family members, in compliance with IPSAS 20.

We recommended that the LCE require the City Accountant and other officials concerned to immediately submit to the COA Audit Team the DVs and supporting documents for the 139 issued checks totaling ₱30,473,245.07.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management:
Cause the timely implementation of the development projects endorsed by City’s Local Development Council through proper coordination with the City Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other Offices involved; and
Conduct thorough evaluation on the feasibility of proposed development projects prior to identification and inclusion in the Annual/Supplemental Investment Plan to preclude delay in project implementation and to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% DF for the attainment of the desirable socio-economic development of the City of Catbalogan.

We recommended that the management refrain from appropriating funds for projects and activities that are not eligible for funding under the 20% DF, ensuring compliance with the DBM-DOF-DILG JM No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that management refrain from appropriating and utilizing LDRRM Fund for the construction/installation of solar street lights in compliance with R.A. No. 10121 and the National DRRM Council, DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1, dated March 25, 2013.

Require the City Engineering Office to assess the cause and extent of the noted deterioration of completed LSGSF-SBDP projects and recommend appropriate corrective measures; and
Require the contractor to implement the corrective measures under the project’s warranty provisions if the deterioration was due to poor workmanship or substandard materials.

ensure preparation and issuance of approved Variation Order for all project modifications, regardless of cost implications; and
submit to the COA Audit Team the approved Variation Orders and supporting documents for technical review and inspection.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the City
Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and intact in compliance with COA
Circular No. 90-331 dated May 3, 1990, COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992
and COA-MOF Joint Circular No. 1-81 dated January 1, 1981.

We recommended that the City stop the practice of granting special cash
advances for programs and projects that are part of a department’s regular
programs so that government funds are efficiently utilized, and the proper
procurement procedures are followed. We also recommended that the LCE require
the accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate all cash advances as
mandated under Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 90-331 dated May 3,
1990 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the LCE instruct all accountable officers of the City
government to follow the proper procedures for all requests for relief from property
accountability in accordance with Section 73 of PD No. 1445 so that government
property is adequately safeguarded.

We recommended that the City require all barangays requesting for financial
assistance to submit the required plans and specifications of the project to be
undertaken and the map indicating where these are to be constructed in compliance
with Section 23 of RA No. 7160, and Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012.

We recommended that procurements for catering services include the
submission of Activity Reports and Inspection Reports for all these
undertakings in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012.

We recommended, in the procurements for infrastructure and in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 17 of Rule VI of
the RIRR of RA No. 9184, that the City require:
(1) The City Accountant, to submit to the Audit Team, supporting documents,
together with the disbursement voucher, for all contracts for infrastructure after
payment has been made;
(2) The Bids and Awards Committee, to submit to the Audit Team, a copy of
the contract with the supporting documents within five days from perfection thereof
to facilitate the legal, auditorial and technical review.

We recommended that the LCE require all barangays who will request for financial
assistance from the City to submit the required plans and specifications of the
project, the map indicating where these projects are to be undertaken and Pre-repair
Inspection Reports, as well as pictures for projects relative to repairs and
rehabilitation, in compliance with Section 23 of RA No. 7160 and Item 3.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Chairman of the BAC to
immediately submit the perfected contract and the required supporting documents
enumerated under COA Circular No. 2009-001, and henceforth, to ensure the
submission of all contracts and their supporting documents within five days after
perfection thereof.

We recommended that the City Accountant see to it that all claims are
supported with complete and proper documentation as required under COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 4 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local School Board follow the proper procedures in
the utilization of the SEF in compliance with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1,
s. 2017. We also recommended that the City Accountant submit complete and proper
documentation to support all SEF disbursements in compliance with COA Circular
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the City Accountant be required to analyze the
transactions covering the line items in the Statement of Financial Position and
reconcile the same with the account details in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We further recommended for the provision of adequate particulars and breakdown for
the account Due to NGAs due to the materiality of its reported balance, pursuant to
paragraphs 127 and 128 of PPSAS No. 1.

We recommended for the City Accountant to revise the combined Statement
of Financial Position and the related Notes to the Financial Statements to include in
its presentation and disclosure the current portion of the long-term debt, pursuant to
paragraphs 70 and 80 of PPSAS No. 1.

We recommend that management present the updated balances in the
Statement of Financial Position for the above-mentioned Property, Plant and
Equipment accounts, pursuant to paragraph 27 of PPSAS No. 1.

We recommend that management comply with the prescribed procedures
and guidelines in the acquisition of private property for public use, as provided
for under the IRR of RA 7160 and the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and
Assessment Operations issued by DOF in January, 2006, and consider the
rescission of the Contract of Sale.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure the
information presented in the RFQs are accurate and obtained from suppliers
undertaking business that are specific to the items to be procured, in order to
promote effective competition in the selection of winning bidders/suppliers,
pursuant to the provisions of RA 9184.

The original copies of the supporting documents, as mentioned above, are
likewise required to be submitted.

We recommend that management include in the MOA, provisions on the return of
funds to LGU-Catbalogan City in the event that the barangay fails to implement
the project within the allotted timeframe without justifiable cause, and/or the
disqualification of delinquent barangay/s from being a recipient of LGU-
Catbalogan City funds for its future projects, by reason of non-implementation of
projects or non-liquidation of received funds.

Management should also designate specific personnel to be assigned to monitor
project implementation and ensure its compliance with technical/engineering
requirements.

We recommend that management discontinue the practice of granting
financial assistance in support of regular operating costs/expenditures of
NGOs/POs, and for activities and programs which do not meet public
interests/purpose.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-
DBM Joint Circular No.2011-1 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the
20% Economic Development Fund for development projects to help achieve
desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend that management judiciously observe the restrictive
conditions provided by the rules and regulations on the use of the Special
Education Fund as provided for in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.
1998 dated April 14, 1998.

We recommend that management post its duly accomplished AAPSI in its
official website, copy furnish the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the
Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 96 of RA 10717.

We recommend that the City General Services Officer be required to undertake measures in managing and validating all PPE items per agency’s books of accounts thru the regular conduct of physical count. We further recommend that the City Accountant be required to strictly utilize the results of the physical count on PPE in reconciling all records maintained in her office as well as that of the GSO.

We recommend that the City Accountant be required to strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF and transfer the unexpected balance of P14,330,447.95 to the Special Trust Fund in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive request the City Division Superintendent to secure authority from the DepEd Regional Director to implement the Senior High School Modelling Program otherwise the use of funds for the implementation of the program should be immediately stopped. Moreover, require the Samar National School Parents-Teachers Association to submit a fund utilization report.

We recommend for the strict observance to the provisions of Sections 99(a) and 100(c) of RA 7160 and DECS/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-B, s. 2001 related in the utilization of SEF.
observations.reason: There were no charges for utility expenses during the second semester of CY 2016. Please see discussion in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC and BAC Secretariat be required to strictly observe all the procurement processes in the conduct of a competitive bidding as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184. Likewise, lacking procurement documents such as: Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and the respective Project Procurement and Management Plan (PPMP); Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC); approved detailed engineering; and evidence of posting of advertisements/notice of wards/notice to proceed in the PhilGEPS, agency website and in the conspicuous place in the premises of the City certified by the Head of the BAC Secretariat should be submitted to the COA audit team.
observations.reason: The BAC had substantially complied with the requirements of RA 9184, 2014 except for the deviations reported in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the City Accountant be required to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 9.1.1.1, Sections 9.1.1.3 to 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, as well as Paragraph 6 of Annex E of RA 9184 to achieve completeness of documentary requirements and economy and effectiveness in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommend that the concerned employees thru the City Accountant be required to submit a properly consolidated work program/plan for a given period and a duly signed individual accomplishment report. Likewise, written explanations why the DTRs attached to the payroll for the payment of overtime services showed different entries with that of the approved DTRs attached to the payroll for salaries as submitted to COA by concerned employees. This recommendation is without prejudice to the results of post-audit once the documents are submitted.

We recommend that the City Mayor ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes, and require the City Planning and Development Office in coordination with the City Budget and City Accounting Department to regularly prepare and submit to COA, the Report on the Utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that Management require the winning bidders to submit the certification from the Insurance Commission and to henceforth, adhere strictly the provisions of Section 39 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommend that management conduct public bidding on the procurement of consulting services and resort to alternative method of procurement only when the conditions for the use of the same are present. Likewise, require the consultants to submit reports, documents and outputs such as training design and modules, schedules of activities, and list of trainers and topics assigned.

We recommend that projects programmed for the year be implemented during the year to maximize the utilization of the fund and ultimately allow the recipients of the projects the use of the facilities and/or projects. As much as practicable, specify the recipients of the projects to facilitate monitoring of the utilization of the fund. It is also recommended that descriptions be consistently used in the budget and in the contracts.
